results.comment_text:
@user1:
Better to include the original message, which will contain " '.0' is not a valid parameter name". 

Also,  it is weird that python2 is generating that a arg with `.0` as the name, and that `funcsigs` can't handle it.

@author:
> Better to include the original message, which will contain " '.0' is not a valid parameter name".

Good idea

> Also, it is weird that python2 is generating that a arg with `.0` as the name, and that `funcsigs` can't handle it.

Yes, this was weird. That you have seen the `.0` confirms it is not just me. Looking at the byte code disassembly, it's definitely in there!
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_pysignature_wraps_valueerror_and_includes_original_message__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/numba/utils.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'from funcsigs import signature as _pysignature' in source, "Expected numba.utils to define a funcsigs-based signature fallback using '_pysignature'."

def test_pysignature_wraps_valueerror_and_includes_original_message__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/numba/utils.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('except\\s+ValueError\\s+as\\s+e\\s*:', source), "pysignature wrapper should capture the ValueError as a variable (expected 'except ValueError as e:') so the original funcsigs message can be included in UnsupportedError."

def test_pysignature_wraps_valueerror_and_includes_original_message__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/numba/utils.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    wrapper_block = re.search('def\\s+pysignature\\s*\\(.*?\\):(?P<body>[\\s\\S]*?)BoundArguments\\.apply_defaults', source)
    assert wrapper_block, 'Could not locate the pysignature wrapper function body in numba.utils.py for inspection.'

def test_pysignature_wraps_valueerror_and_includes_original_message__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/numba/utils.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    wrapper_block = re.search('def\\s+pysignature\\s*\\(.*?\\):(?P<body>[\\s\\S]*?)BoundArguments\\.apply_defaults', source)
    body = wrapper_block.group('body')
    assert 'e.message' in body or re.search('\\bstr\\s*\\(\\s*e\\s*\\)', body) or re.search('%\\s*\\(\\s*[^)]*\\be\\b[^)]*\\)', body) or re.search('%\\s*[^%\\n]*\\be\\b', body), "pysignature wrapper should include the original funcsigs ValueError text in the UnsupportedError message (expected to reference the caught exception e.g. via 'e.message' or 'str(e)')."
